body: Describe the ultimate beach trip you'd take this summer.
subtext: Where would you go? Who would you take? Write as much or as little as you want. A sentence, a story, or anything in between... but more is better!

body: If you could spend a whole day doing just one thing, what would it be?
subtext: Go as specific as you can — what does the whole day actually look like?

body: Write about someone who inspired you recently — could be anyone.
subtext: It doesn't have to be a big dramatic moment.

body: Invent a new holiday. What's it called, and how do people celebrate it?
subtext: The weirder the better.

body: You wake up and discover you have one superpower for exactly 24 hours. What is it and what do you do?
Describe a sound that makes you feel completely peaceful.
subtext: Try to describe it so well that someone who's never heard it can imagine it.

body: What's something you changed your mind about recently?
subtext: Could be something big or something small.

body: Write the opening paragraph of a story that starts with: "The summer I found the map..."
subtext: It can go anywhere — realistic, fantasy, mystery, whatever you want.

body: Describe your perfect Saturday morning in as much detail as possible.
subtext: What do you hear, smell, eat? Who's there?

body: If you could swap lives with anyone for a week — real or fictional — who would it be and why?
What's the bravest thing you've done this year?
subtext: Big or small — bravery is bravery.

body: Describe a place you've never been but have always wanted to visit.
subtext: What does it look, sound, and smell like in your imagination?

body: Write a letter to yourself to open at the end of summer.
subtext: What do you hope you'll have done? What do you want to remember?

body: What's something you're really good at that most people don't know about?
If you could have dinner with anyone — living, historical, or fictional — who would it be, and what would you ask them?
Describe a moment this summer when you felt completely happy.
subtext: Even a tiny ordinary moment counts.

body: Make up a creature that doesn't exist. Describe what it looks like, where it lives, and what it eats.
subtext: The stranger the better.

body: What's one thing you'd change about the world if you could?
subtext: Why that, and how would it work?

body: Describe a skill or hobby you'd love to learn. What's stopping you?
Write about a friendship — what makes it special, and what's one memory that shows exactly why.
If you directed a movie about your life so far, what would the title be and who would play you?
subtext: Write the one-sentence plot summary too.

body: What's the best piece of advice you've ever received?
subtext: Who gave it to you, and did you actually follow it?

body: Describe a time you surprised yourself.
If you could live in any time period for one year, when would you choose and why?
subtext: Think about what you'd gain — and what you'd miss.

body: What's something you're looking forward to about the school year ahead?
subtext: Even one tiny thing counts.

body: Write about your favorite thing you ate this summer.
subtext: Describe it like you're trying to make someone hungry just by reading.

body: What's a question you've been thinking about that you don't have an answer to yet?
subtext: It can be about anything — big or small.

body: Describe your dream house. Where is it, what does it look like, and what's in it?
What's something you did this summer that you're proud of?
subtext: Doesn't have to be a big achievement.

body: If your summer had a soundtrack, what songs would be on it?
subtext: Pick three and say why each one fits.
